skill.name: plan-harder
skill.description: Creates detailed, phased implementation plans when the user explicitly asks to plan harder.
skill.summary: Builds sprint-based implementation plans with research, validation steps, risks, and review.
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution and ordinary text as weak cryptography. Manual review of SKILL.md found no executable scripts, network access, credential handling, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Lines 71, 74, 75, and 92 use markdown formatting for a filename suffix, filename examples, and a fenced template block. They do not instruct the agent to execute Ruby, shell commands, or any external command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is markdown syntax and filename examples. No command string, interpreter invocation, subprocess API, or executable script is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Misread
low_findings.description: Lines 3, 88, and 113 contain frontmatter text, planning guidance, and a template label. Manual review found no hash functions, encryption code, signatures, or cryptographic configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural language and markdown template content. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic use.

content.value_statement: Complex engineering work can fail when requirements, dependencies, and validation are unclear. This skill creates phased plans with research, atomic tasks, risk review, and verification steps.
actual_capabilities: Guides codebase research before plan creation.
Prompts for missing requirements and scope boundaries.
Breaks work into phased sprints and atomic tasks.
Adds validation, demo, dependency, and acceptance criteria to tasks.
Saves a plan using a kebab-case filename ending in -plan.md.
Reviews gotchas and can request subagent feedback before finalizing.
limitations: The skill is designed for planning, not implementation.
It activates only when the user explicitly asks to plan harder.
Plan quality depends on available repository and requirement context.
Subagent review depends on the host tool supporting subagents.

use_cases.title: Feature Planning
use_cases.description: Turn a large feature request into scoped sprints with clear tasks, dependencies, and validation steps.

use_cases.title: Bug Fix Planning
use_cases.description: Investigate a complex defect and create a plan that covers root cause, tests, rollout, and rollback.

use_cases.title: Technical Lead Review
use_cases.description: Prepare an implementation roadmap that exposes risks, open questions, and review checkpoints before work starts.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Small Fix
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a focused change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan harder for fixing this bug. Research the relevant files, ask any needed questions, and create a phased plan only.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate planning for a user-facing feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan harder for implementing this feature. Include scope, dependencies, sprints, acceptance criteria, tests, and risks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Migration
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for multi-step technical work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan harder for this migration. Include repository research, compatibility risks, rollout stages, rollback steps, and validation per sprint.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Cross-Team Work
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning with dependencies and review needs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan harder for this cross-team project. Identify ownership boundaries, sequencing, blockers, parallel work, review gates, and release checks.

output_examples.input: Plan harder for adding Google sign-in to the app.
output_examples.output: A saved plan with research notes, OAuth configuration tasks, UI tasks, callback handling, tests, demo checks, risks, and rollback notes.

output_examples.input: Plan harder for fixing intermittent checkout failures.
output_examples.output: A phased debugging plan covering reproduction, logging, root cause analysis, targeted fixes, regression tests, and deployment validation.

output_examples.input: Plan harder for migrating the frontend build tool.
output_examples.output: A sprint plan that separates dependency audit, configuration changes, compatibility fixes, test updates, release checks, and rollback planning.

best_practices: Provide the feature, bug, or migration goal with current constraints.
Share known risks, deadlines, owners, and success criteria early.
Review the generated plan before asking for implementation work.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill when you need immediate code changes.
Do not skip requirement clarification for ambiguous work.
Do not treat the plan as final without reviewing repository-specific assumptions.

faq.question: Does this skill write code?
faq.answer: No. It creates an implementation plan and explicitly avoids making code changes.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when work is complex enough to need research, sequencing, validation, and risk review.

faq.question: Can it ask clarifying questions?
faq.answer: Yes. It instructs the agent to ask targeted questions when requirements or scope are unclear.

faq.question: What does the saved plan include?
faq.answer: It includes overview, prerequisites, sprints, atomic tasks, validation, risks, and rollback guidance.

faq.question: Does it support codebase-specific planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It begins by researching architecture, existing patterns, dependencies, and related components.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Manual review found no executable commands, network behavior, credential access, or prompt injection.
